pages.desc: A Khom-script (ขอม) primer chart — the opening page of a section headed แบบเรียนเขียนขอม โดยสังเขป ("a concise primer on writing Khom"), within the treatise ตำราสร้างเครื่องรางของขลัง. Below the headi
pages.descFull: A Khom-script (ขอม) primer chart — the opening page of a section headed แบบเรียนเขียนขอม โดยสังเขป ("a concise primer on writing Khom"), within the treatise ตำราสร้างเครื่องรางของขลัง. Below the heading a line of Khom sacred script is glossed in Thai as นะ โม พุท ธา ยะ สิท ธัง (Namo Buddhāya… siddhaṃ), the auspicious opening invocation. The body is a syllabary table: hand-drawn Khom vowel and consonant glyphs in black ink, each paired beneath with its Thai phonetic reading — the vowels อะ อา อิ อี อุ อู เอ ไอ across the top, then the consonant series กะ ขะ คะ ฆะ งะ… running down to อัง ออ. A teaching page for the sacred alphabet in which katha and yantra are inscribed, set out glyph by glyph.

pages.desc: A Khom-script (ขอม) spelling chart headed แจกแม่กะกา ('spelling out the แม่ กะกา syllable class'), continuing the sacred-alphabet primer of the earlier pages. It is a large grid in which every cell pa
pages.descFull: A Khom-script (ขอม) spelling chart headed แจกแม่กะกา ('spelling out the แม่ กะกา syllable class'), continuing the sacred-alphabet primer of the earlier pages. It is a large grid in which every cell pairs a hand-inked Khom syllable-glyph with its Thai phonetic reading printed beneath. The top row runs the twelve vowel forms of the ก consonant — กะ กา กิ กี กุ กู เก ไก กุง กิง กัง กอ — and each row below repeats the same twelve patterns for the next consonant of the alphabet (ขะ, คะ, งะ, จะ, ชะ …). Running header ตำราสร้างเครื่องรางของขลัง; page number หน้า ๒ at the foot.

pages.desc: A continuation of the Khom (ขอม) แม่กะกา spelling grid, carried on without a new heading. Same layout: each cell shows a Khom syllable-glyph above its Thai phonetic reading. The twelve columns keep th
pages.descFull: A continuation of the Khom (ขอม) แม่กะกา spelling grid, carried on without a new heading. Same layout: each cell shows a Khom syllable-glyph above its Thai phonetic reading. The twelve columns keep the vowel pattern (–ะ –า –ิ –ี –ุ –ู เ– โ– –ุง –ิง –ัง –อ) while successive rows advance through further consonants — legible first-column readings include ทะ, ณะ, ตะ, ถะ, ธะ, นะ, ปะ, ผะ, พะ and ภะ. Running header ตำราสร้างเครื่องรางของขลัง; foot of page หน้า ๓.

pages.desc: The closing rows of the Khom (ขอม) แม่กะกา spelling grid, still without a heading. Each cell carries a Khom syllable-glyph over its Thai reading, the twelve columns holding the same vowel sequence (–ะ
pages.descFull: The closing rows of the Khom (ขอม) แม่กะกา spelling grid, still without a heading. Each cell carries a Khom syllable-glyph over its Thai reading, the twelve columns holding the same vowel sequence (–ะ –า –ิ –ี –ุ –ู เ– โ– –ุง –ิง –ัง –อ). The rows here cover the final consonants of the series — legible first-column readings run มะ, ยะ, ระ, ละ, วะ, สะ, หะ and พะ. The lower half of the page is blank. Running header ตำราสร้างเครื่องรางของขลัง; foot หน้า ๔.

pages.desc: A Khom-script (ขอม) primer page divided into four spelling exercises, each with its own heading and a block of twelve syllables (two rows of six) pairing a Khom glyph with its Thai reading. The sectio
pages.descFull: A Khom-script (ขอม) primer page divided into four spelling exercises, each with its own heading and a block of twelve syllables (two rows of six) pairing a Khom glyph with its Thai reading. The sections are headed แจกแม่กันนะ, แจกแม่กัณณะ, แจกแม่กัพพะ and แจกแม่กัลละ — each 'spelling out' a doubled or clustered consonant through the vowels, e.g. กันนะ กันนา กันนิ กันนี กันนุ กันนู / กันเน กันโน กันนุง กันนิง กันนัง กันนอ. Running header ตำราสร้างเครื่องรางของขลัง; foot หน้า ๕.

pages.desc: A Khom-script (ขอม) primer page with four spelling exercises, each headed and holding twelve syllables (two rows of six) — a Khom glyph above its Thai reading. Headings: แจกแม่กัปปะ, แจกแม่กัพพะ, แจกแ
pages.descFull: A Khom-script (ขอม) primer page with four spelling exercises, each headed and holding twelve syllables (two rows of six) — a Khom glyph above its Thai reading. Headings: แจกแม่กัปปะ, แจกแม่กัพพะ, แจกแม่กัตตะ and แจกแม่กัจจะ, each running a clustered consonant through the vowel forms, e.g. กัปปะ กัปปา กัปปิ กัปปี กัปปุ กัปปู / กัปเป กัปโป กัปปุง กัปปิง กัปปัง กัปปอ. Running header ตำราสร้างเครื่องรางของขลัง; foot หน้า ๖.

pages.desc: A Khom-script (ขอม) primer page of four spelling exercises, each headed and holding twelve syllables (two rows of six) pairing a Khom glyph with its Thai reading. Headings: แจกแม่กัชชะ, แจกแม่กัญจะ, แ
pages.descFull: A Khom-script (ขอม) primer page of four spelling exercises, each headed and holding twelve syllables (two rows of six) pairing a Khom glyph with its Thai reading. Headings: แจกแม่กัชชะ, แจกแม่กัญจะ, แจกแม่กักกะ and แจกแม่กัคคะ — each spelling a doubled or clustered consonant through the vowels, e.g. กักกะ กักกา กักกิ กักกี กักกุ กักกู / กักเก กักโก กักกุง กักกิง กักกัง กักกอ. Running header ตำราสร้างเครื่องรางของขลัง; foot หน้า ๗.

pages.desc: A Khom-script (ขอม) primer page with three spelling exercises in the upper half, each headed and holding twelve syllables (two rows of six) — a Khom glyph over its Thai reading. Headings: แจกแม่กังงะ,
pages.descFull: A Khom-script (ขอม) primer page with three spelling exercises in the upper half, each headed and holding twelve syllables (two rows of six) — a Khom glyph over its Thai reading. Headings: แจกแม่กังงะ, แจกแม่กัมมะ and แจกแม่กัยยะ, each running a clustered consonant through the vowel forms, e.g. กัมมะ กัมมา กัมมิ กัมมี กัมมุ กัมมู / กัมเม กัมโม กัมมุง กัมมิง กัมมัง กัมมอ. The lower half of the page is blank. Running header ตำราสร้างเครื่องรางของขลัง; foot หน้า ๘.

pages.desc: A large square yant (ยันต์) whose cells are filled with hand-drawn Khom (ขอม) sacred syllables in black ink, roughly a nine-by-nine grid. Rising above the square is a tiered temple-spire (prasat-like)
pages.descFull: A large square yant (ยันต์) whose cells are filled with hand-drawn Khom (ขอม) sacred syllables in black ink, roughly a nine-by-nine grid. Rising above the square is a tiered temple-spire (prasat-like) crown of stepped registers, each capped by small stupa finials trailing thin antenna-spires. Six-pointed star/rosette motifs with dotted nodes mark the four corners, and a stupa emblem is centered on each side. The caption beneath reads ยันต์นี้ ให้ลงผ้าสีชมพูห้อยหน้าที่พระฉิมพลีแล ('this yant is to be inscribed on pink cloth and hung in front of Phra Chimphli/Sivali'). Printed page ๑๘.

pages.desc: A tiered, pyramid-shaped yant built as three stacked panels of Khom (ขอม) syllable cells, each panel enclosing a seated figure. At the apex, within a pointed flame-shaped arch, sits a Buddha figure wi
pages.descFull: A tiered, pyramid-shaped yant built as three stacked panels of Khom (ขอม) syllable cells, each panel enclosing a seated figure. At the apex, within a pointed flame-shaped arch, sits a Buddha figure with a pointed ushnisha; a second seated figure occupies the middle tier; and the lowest figure sits cross-legged with both arms raised above the head. Columns of Khom letters flank every tier, and the base register holds three small square yant with diagonal cross/star motifs. The caption reads ยันต์นี้ ให้ลงผนังหลังที่พระฉิมพลีแล ('this yant is to be inscribed on the back wall at Phra Chimphli/Sivali'). Printed page ๑๙.

pages.desc: A tall, multi-tiered yant drawn as a stepped temple spire (prasat), its stacked registers filled with Khom (ขอม) syllables interspersed with small stupa finials. The structure tapers to a pointed pinn
pages.descFull: A tall, multi-tiered yant drawn as a stepped temple spire (prasat), its stacked registers filled with Khom (ขอม) syllables interspersed with small stupa finials. The structure tapers to a pointed pinnacle crowned by a square-and-star motif above a stupa, from which thin antenna-spires rise; further small square star-yant and pendant tasselled lines float symmetrically to either side, with columns of Khom letters running down the flanks. The caption reads ยันต์นี้ให้ทำเป็นธงไว้หน้าที่พระฉิมพลีแล ('this yant is to be made into a banner/flag placed in front of Phra Chimphli/Sivali'). Printed page ๒๐.

pages.desc: A yant composed of three tall vertical bands. The outer two are lattice chains of lozenge cells (X-crossed netting), each cell holding a Khom (ขอม) syllable, their long edges lined with continuous col
pages.descFull: A yant composed of three tall vertical bands. The outer two are lattice chains of lozenge cells (X-crossed netting), each cell holding a Khom (ขอม) syllable, their long edges lined with continuous columns of Khom script; the central band stacks a square panel, a diamond-in-square figure, and a small grid, all of Khom letters, topped by tiered stupa finials on thin spires. Along the base run three diamond-in-square yant, each with a line of Khom text beneath it. The caption reads ยันต์นี้ลงผ้าแดงทำเป็นม่านที่พระฉิมพลีแล ('this yant is inscribed on red cloth made into a curtain at Phra Chimphli/Sivali'). Printed page ๒๑.

pages.desc: Two yant on one page. The upper figure is a square panel enclosing a diagonally-set diamond grid of Khom (ขอม) syllables, its outer edges scalloped with looping lotus-petal curves and small tiered mot
pages.descFull: Two yant on one page. The upper figure is a square panel enclosing a diagonally-set diamond grid of Khom (ขอม) syllables, its outer edges scalloped with looping lotus-petal curves and small tiered motifs at the corners; its caption reads ยันต์นี้ลงเพดานที่พระฉิมพลีแล ('this yant is inscribed on the ceiling at Phra Chimphli/Sivali'). The lower figure is a square grid of Khom syllable cells with a small diamond at its center and flame-like rays shooting from the corners and sides; its caption reads ยันต์นี้ลงกรรชิงที่พระฉิมพลีแล เอาไม้ง้วมาทำเป็นกรรชิงกางแล ('this yant is inscribed on the ceremonial parasol (กรรชิง) at Phra Chimphli/Sivali; take ngua wood to make the spread parasol'). Printed page ๒๒.

pages.desc: Two figures under the heading ยันต์เทียนพราชปุตโต ('candle yant, Racha-putto'). The upper yant rises from an interlaced double-loop (a pretzel-like knotted band) whose crossing straps each carry a Kho
pages.descFull: Two figures under the heading ยันต์เทียนพราชปุตโต ('candle yant, Racha-putto'). The upper yant rises from an interlaced double-loop (a pretzel-like knotted band) whose crossing straps each carry a Khom (ขอม) syllable; above it a pointed arch holds numerals, then two rows of sacred Khom syllables, capped by a spiral flame-stroke (unalome/อุณาโลม) tipped with a small circle. Below sits a rectangular magic-number square: an outer ring of bordered cells framing an inner block, all filled with Thai numerals (e.g. ๘ ๓ ๗ ๘, ๖ ๕ ๒ ๖, ๑ ๓...). Its caption reads ยันต์นี้ลงข้างหน้า ('this yant is inscribed on the front'). Printed page ๒๖.

pages.desc: Section heading ตำราสร้างประคำ ๗ ลูก ('treatise on making a 7-bead rosary'). Seven gourd- or vase-shaped yant are arranged in a pyramid — one at the top, then two rows of three. Each figure holds Khom
pages.descFull: Section heading ตำราสร้างประคำ ๗ ลูก ('treatise on making a 7-bead rosary'). Seven gourd- or vase-shaped yant are arranged in a pyramid — one at the top, then two rows of three. Each figure holds Khom (ขอม) sacred syllables in its bulb, is crowned by a flame-like unalome spire with radiating strokes and small syllables at the shoulders, carries the Thai numerals ๑ ๒ ๓ ๔ beneath, and ends in a small bow-tie / figure-eight motif. The caption สิทธิการิยะ directs that these seven Itipiso yant (ยันต์พระอิติปิโส) be cast in lead as rosary beads, one yant per bead, then consecrated 108 times. Page ๑๐๐.

pages.desc: Section heading ตำราสร้างประคำพระเจ้ากลืนไตรภพ ('making the rosary of the Buddha who swallows the three worlds'). At left stand three large gourd-shaped yant, each ringed by radiating spokes that term
pages.descFull: Section heading ตำราสร้างประคำพระเจ้ากลืนไตรภพ ('making the rosary of the Buddha who swallows the three worlds'). At left stand three large gourd-shaped yant, each ringed by radiating spokes that terminate in small Khom (ขอม) syllables and Thai numerals, with vertical Thai labels beside them (…ลูกน้อยยอดแล). At right, a tall stacked column of small bell- or lotus-shaped bead figures, each flanked by Khom letters and short unalome-like strokes, forms a numbered set of rosary beads. Page ๑๐๑.

pages.desc: A continuation of the rosary-bead catalogue. Rows of small, near-identical amulet figures fill two columns; each is a bell- or lotus-shaped medallion flanked left and right by Khom (ขอม) sacred syllab
pages.descFull: A continuation of the rosary-bead catalogue. Rows of small, near-identical amulet figures fill two columns; each is a bell- or lotus-shaped medallion flanked left and right by Khom (ขอม) sacred syllables and finished with short zig-zag unalome strokes. Every figure bears a Thai numeral to its left, numbering the beads in sequence, and several carry extra crossed (X) strokes radiating from top and bottom. No running prose — a plate of repeating amulet diagrams. Page ๑๐๒.

pages.desc: More of the numbered bead series. The page holds two columns of small, uniform medallion figures — each a lotus/bell form with Khom (ขอม) syllables set to either side and slender unalome tails above a
pages.descFull: More of the numbered bead series. The page holds two columns of small, uniform medallion figures — each a lotus/bell form with Khom (ขอม) syllables set to either side and slender unalome tails above and below, several with crossed (X) accent strokes. A Thai numeral labels each bead, continuing the running count of the rosary set. Purely diagrammatic, with no explanatory text. Page ๑๐๓.

pages.desc: A further plate in the same series: two columns of small bell-and-lotus bead figures, each with paired Khom (ขอม) syllables at the sides, unalome-like flourishes at top and bottom, and a Thai numeral
pages.descFull: A further plate in the same series: two columns of small bell-and-lotus bead figures, each with paired Khom (ขอม) syllables at the sides, unalome-like flourishes at top and bottom, and a Thai numeral marking its place in the sequence. Some figures add crossed (X) strokes flanking the body. The forms stay consistent across the page, cataloguing successive beads of the charm-rosary. Page ๑๐๔.

pages.desc: Another catalogue plate of the numbered rosary beads. Two columns of small, uniform medallion figures fill the sheet — each a lotus/bell shape carrying Khom (ขอม) syllables on both flanks and thin una
pages.descFull: Another catalogue plate of the numbered rosary beads. Two columns of small, uniform medallion figures fill the sheet — each a lotus/bell shape carrying Khom (ขอม) syllables on both flanks and thin unalome strokes above and below, several with crossed (X) marks. Each bears its own Thai numeral, continuing the ordered set. No prose accompanies the figures. Page ๑๐๕.

pages.desc: The bead-series plate concludes here. Most of the page is again two columns of small numbered medallion figures with paired Khom (ขอม) syllables and unalome strokes. At the upper right sit two distinc
pages.descFull: The bead-series plate concludes here. Most of the page is again two columns of small numbered medallion figures with paired Khom (ขอม) syllables and unalome strokes. At the upper right sit two distinct yant: a circle quartered by a diagonal cross (X), each of the four cells holding a single Khom syllable, and below it a four-lobed leaf / quatrefoil form with Khom syllables in each lobe and at its centre. A short vertical Khom line labels them. Page ๑๐๖.

pages.desc: Section heading ตำราสร้างตะกรุดหนังเสือ แบบที่ ๑ ('treatise on making a tiger-skin takrut, type 1'). The upper figure is a large square yant grid set on the diagonal (a diamond), its cells packed with
pages.descFull: Section heading ตำราสร้างตะกรุดหนังเสือ แบบที่ ๑ ('treatise on making a tiger-skin takrut, type 1'). The upper figure is a large square yant grid set on the diagonal (a diamond), its cells packed with Khom (ขอม) syllables, arrows projecting outward at the four points and small stupa-like caps studding the border; it is captioned ยันต์นี้ลงข้างหน้า ('inscribe this yant on the front'). Below is a line drawing of a walking lion (ราชสีห์) with a flame-like mane and Khom on its body, captioned ราชสีห์ตัวนี้ลงข้างหลัง ('this lion on the back'). The prose directs scraping the tiger hide bare before inscribing. Page ๑๔๒.

pages.desc: Section heading ตำราสร้างธงพระจุฬามณี ('treatise on making the Phra Chulamani banner'). The drawing shows a tall flagpole flying two large triangular pennants, one above the other, each ruled into a g
pages.descFull: Section heading ตำราสร้างธงพระจุฬามณี ('treatise on making the Phra Chulamani banner'). The drawing shows a tall flagpole flying two large triangular pennants, one above the other, each ruled into a grid of Khom (ขอม) syllables with rows of repeated letters and a line of Khom katha running along the top edge. To the left hangs a separate fish- or rocket-shaped banner (a ธง / tung) whose long body is a vertical grid of Khom syllables, finished with decorative fins and small pennant tails. Page ๑๕๕.
